tagline: Work with Jira Cloud issues, projects, and users from the command line.

global_flags.name: connection
global_flags.summary: Use a saved Jira connector credential and site scope.

flags.name: jql
flags.summary: Filter issues with Jira Query Language.

flags.name: fields
flags.summary: Comma-separated Jira issue fields to request.

flags.name: expand
flags.summary: Comma-separated Jira issue expansions to request.

commands.notes: Executes the existing Jira issues stream. Single-issue rendering is deferred to a later direct-read slice.

commands.summary: Search issues with Jira query parameters
commands.notes: The connector has an issue-search stream, but gh-style/Jira-style ad-hoc JQL flags are not yet exposed as command flags.
Requires a bounded direct-read executor for caller-supplied issue IDs.

commands.approval: Future reverse ETL writes must use plan, preview, approval, and execute.
commands.notes: No Jira write action is declared in this metadata slice.

commands.approval: Future reverse ETL writes must use plan, preview, approval, and execute.
commands.notes: Blocked until #107 classifies the operation and #110 defines sensitive/admin policy.

commands.summary: Transition an issue through a Jira workflow
commands.risk: Changes issue workflow state and may trigger Jira automation.
commands.approval: Future reverse ETL writes must use plan, preview, approval, and execute.
commands.notes: Workflow transitions require explicit typed approval and field validation before execution.
Issue deletion is destructive and is blocked by default.
Requires per-issue fan-out or a bounded direct-read command for caller-supplied issue IDs.

commands.risk: Adds visible comment text to a Jira issue.
commands.approval: Future reverse ETL writes must use plan, preview, approval, and execute.
commands.notes: No comment write action is declared in this metadata slice.
Requires per-issue fan-out or a bounded direct-read command for caller-supplied issue IDs.

commands.risk: Writes time-tracking data to Jira issues.
commands.approval: Any future support must use plan, preview, approval, and execute with typed confirmation.
commands.notes: Time-tracking writes are sensitive business records and remain blocked by default.
Structured attachment metadata is a candidate for a bounded direct-read or fan-out stream after the operation ledger slice.
Attachment content returns binary payloads and needs an explicit bounded file-output policy before enabling.

flags.name: type-key
flags.summary: Filter projects by project type key.

flags.name: status
flags.summary: Filter projects by Jira lifecycle status.

commands.notes: Requires a bounded direct-read executor for caller-supplied project keys or IDs.
Project creation is an administrative action and is blocked by default.
Project deletion is destructive admin and is not exposed.
Project subresources need project-ID fan-out or bounded direct reads.
Project version reads need project-ID fan-out or bounded direct reads.

commands.summary: List Jira users visible to the site

flags.name: query
flags.summary: Filter users by display name, email, or account text.

flags.name: account-id
flags.summary: Filter users by Jira account ID.

flags.name: username
flags.summary: Legacy username filter for Jira sites that still accept it.

commands.notes: Requires a bounded direct-read executor for caller-supplied account IDs.
The endpoint is currently used as the connector health check and is not yet exposed as a command.
Group APIs require elevated permissions on many sites and need operation-ledger risk classification.
Jira Software Agile APIs are outside the current REST v3 legacy stream set and need a separate ledger row.
Requires Agile API coverage and board-ID scoping.
Requires Agile API or JQL-backed operation design; not part of the current legacy streams.
Workflow metadata commonly requires admin permissions and needs #107/#110 classification.
Workflow mutations are administrative and blocked by default.
Field metadata is useful but outside the current issue/project/user legacy stream set.
Permission scheme APIs are admin-oriented and need elevated-scope classification.
Screen configuration APIs are admin-oriented and need elevated-scope classification.
Webhook APIs require explicit permission/risk review before command exposure.
Use `pm credentials add jira ...` with environment variables, stdin, or secret manager input; do not pass secrets in prompt text or examples.
Connector metadata does not write local CLI config. Use Polymetrics connection and credential commands.

commands.summary: Call an arbitrary Jira REST endpoint
commands.notes: Generic raw Jira API calls are intentionally not exposed. Use typed streams, direct reads, or reverse ETL actions only.

help_topics.name: auth
help_topics.summary: Use Polymetrics credentials for Jira site URL, email, and API token; never place secrets in command examples.

help_topics.name: writes
help_topics.summary: Jira writes are metadata-only in this slice and remain blocked until reverse ETL policy and operation ledger slices are complete.

help_topics.name: attachments
help_topics.summary: Attachment content is binary and requires explicit output bounds before any download executor can be enabled.
